items.headline: Auto sales dealership proposed for El Camino Real location
items.summary: Mahsa Nicknam applied for a special development permit to operate an auto sales business at 975 W. El Camino Real in the ECR-C commercial zone. The project involves lot improvements like repaving and restriping but no building expansion or exterior changes.

items.headline: City launches financial assistance pilot for water customers
items.summary: San José City Council approved a Financial Assistance Pilot Program to help water customers with their bills. The program aims to support residents facing water service affordability challenges.

items.headline: City faces potential lawsuit over Cuesta Park water main incident
items.summary: Mountain View's legal team is preparing for possible litigation stemming from a water main failure at Cuesta Park. The closed session discussion suggests the city is evaluating its liability and next steps regarding the incident.

items.headline: Planning Commission considers 27-unit townhome project with affordable units
items.summary: The Planning Commission reviewed a residential development proposal for 27 three-story townhomes, including 5 affordable units. This mixed-income housing project represents a notable addition to Cupertino's residential stock.

items.headline: City Council to hear 72-unit affordable housing project on Lytton Avenue
items.summary: The Council will hold a public hearing on a six-story, 100% affordable housing development at 450 Lytton Avenue that would replace a city-owned surface parking lot (Lot T) with 72 residential units. The project is requesting density bonus concessions and waivers under state law.

items.headline: Palo Alto permits major electrical upgrade for Porter Drive R&D tenant
items.summary: A building permit has been issued for significant electrical infrastructure improvements at 3210 Porter Drive, including new transformers and a substation connection along Porter Drive to support expanded research and development operations. The project involves substantial upgrades to serve new tenant improvements at the existing facility.

items.headline: City Council adopts policy restricting federal immigration enforcement on city property
items.summary: Campbell adopted a resolution prohibiting use of city-owned and city-operated property for federal civil immigration enforcement actions. This policy limits cooperation between local facilities and federal immigration authorities.

items.headline: City declares hazardous vegetation a public nuisance
items.summary: Los Gatos adopted a resolution classifying overgrown brush and hazardous vegetation as a public nuisance, enabling the city to enforce removal and order property owners to clear dangerous brush. This typically applies to fire risk mitigation and can result in city-ordered cleanup with costs passed to property owners.
